codeReview.summary: No code changes were required for this feature. The worker correctly identified that the existing nginx.conf already routes /prices and /check_bucket endpoints to all four chain containers via the generic location /{chain}/ blocks. The trailing slash on proxy_pass (e.g., `proxy_pass http://ypm-ethereum:8001/;`) causes nginx to strip the /{chain}/ prefix and forward the remainder to the backend — so /ethereum/prices becomes /prices, /ethereum/check_bucket becomes /check_bucket, etc. This is standard nginx URI rewriting behavior and was verified by the worker through comprehensive curl tests against rebuilt Docker containers. All four chains (ethereum, optimism, base confirmed 200; arbitrum confirmed via /health due to RPC timeouts), invalid chain (/polygon/) confirmed 404, and existing routes (/, /health, /price) confirmed unaffected.

issues.description: The proxy_read_timeout is set to 120s for chain location blocks, but the worker experienced curl timeouts (exit code 28) on arbitrum endpoints even with --max-time 120. Since curl's max-time and nginx's proxy_read_timeout are both 120s, the curl timeout fires at the same time as nginx would timeout. The worker correctly diagnosed this as an RPC/ypricemagic issue rather than a routing issue, as /arbitrum/health returned 200. No action needed, but the tight timeout margin could cause ambiguous failures in manual testing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The commit ID in the handoff (64693b5) references the check_bucket endpoint commit, not a nginx-specific commit. Since no code changes were needed, this is acceptable, but the convention for verification-only features that produce no commits is not documented in AGENTS.md or the skill.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff commitId: 64693b5, but `git show 64693b5` is 'feat: add GET /check_bucket endpoint for token classification' — not nginx-related. The worker made no code changes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill procedure says 'Write Tests First (TDD)' and 'Commit all changes' as mandatory steps, but verification-only features that require no code changes don't fit this workflow. The worker correctly skipped these steps. The skill could benefit from a note about verification-only features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill SKILL.md steps 3 (Write Tests First), 4 (Implement), 7 (Commit) were all skipped because the feature was verification-only. skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true despite skipping these steps.

summary: The nginx routing verification passes. The worker correctly determined that no nginx.conf changes were needed — the existing location /{chain}/ blocks with trailing-slash proxy_pass already handle prefix stripping, so /prices and /check_bucket route correctly to all four chain containers. Comprehensive curl testing confirmed: ethereum/optimism/base returned 200 for both endpoints, arbitrum routing works (health returned 200; data endpoints timed out due to RPC, not routing), invalid chain (polygon) returned 404, and all existing routes (/, /health, /price) are unaffected. All validators (pytest 205 passed, mypy clean, ruff clean) passed.
